case_title: PATRICIA LUZI et al VS. 1905 LAGUNA STREET CONDOMINIUM HOMEOMERS' et al

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MARK TANGARD AND JOHN KEATING'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, ALLEGATIONS CONCERNING PUNITIVE DAMAGES, PROPERTY DAMAGE ALLEGATIONS, BODILY INJURY ALLEGATION AND PRAYER FOR GENERAL DAMAGES. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED EACH OF THE GROUNDS ALLEGED. STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS ARGUMENT IS NOT CONTROLLING. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS ESTABLISHING BODILY INJURY TO SUPPORT A CLAIM FOR EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. SEE MERCADO VERSUS LEONG (1996) 43 CAL. APP. 4TH 317, 324. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: JOANN PRIOR, CSR 	129

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS 1905 LAGUNA STREET CONDOMINIUM HOMEOWNERS', MARK TANGARD, STEVE BARTHOLOMEW AND JOHN KEATING'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: 1. OVERRULED AS TO THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED AN ON-GOING CONTRACTUAL OBLIGATION. SEE ROMANO VERSUS ROCKWELL INTERNATIONAL INC., 14 CAL. 4TH 479, 489 (1996). 2. OVERRULED AS TO FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION. NEGLIGENCE ALLEGED TO BE ON-GOING AND DISTINCT EVENTS. AT BEST, ONLY PART OF THE CLAIM IS BARRED. 3. SUSTAIN WITH LEAVE TO AMEND THE TENTH AND ELEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION TO ALLEGE FACTS RELATED TO DEFENDANTS' BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY. CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 425.15 DOES APPLY TO THE ASSOCIATION. 4. SUSTAIN WITH LEAVE TO AMEND THE NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION. CIVIL CODE SECTION 1941 IS INAPPLICABLE; CIVIL CODE SECTION 1964 DOES NOT EXIST; PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS ESTABLISHING A BASIS FOR A CLAIM OF NUISANCE. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: JOANN PRIOR, CSR 	129

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Nov-12-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Stephen K. Lightfooot, Esq. and Frank G. Adam, Esq. appeared with plaintiffs Patricia Luzi, Alex Hautau & Steve Gendreau, all of whom were placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendant Walter Rogers, individually and dba Pacific Coastal Reserves.
Default was entered against defendant Walter Rogers, individuall and dba Pacific Coastal Reserves on November 6, 2009.
All other defendants named in the second amended complaint, including doe defendants, were with prejudice dismissed today, November 12, 2009.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs Patricia Luzi, Alex Huatau & Steve Gendreau, and against the Defaulted Defendant Walter Rogers, individually and dba Pacific Coastal Reserves, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $434,000.00.
Costs - $5,456.93, for a total judgment in the amount of $439,456.93.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Default Prove-Up Hearing.

MOTION To Strike The Prayer For Punitive Damages,Allegations Concerning Punitive Damages, Property Damage Allegations, Bodily Injury Allegation And Prayer For General Damages
Off Calendar May-21-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS MARK TANGARD AND JOHN KEATING'S MOTION To Strike The Prayer For Punitive Damages, Allegations Concerning Punitive Damages, Property Damage Allegations, Bodily Injury Allegation And Prayer For General Damages.
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED EACH OF THE GROUNDS ALLEGED. STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS ARGUMENT IS NOT CONTROLLING. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS ESTABLISHING BODILY INJURY TO SUPPORT A CLAIM FOR EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. SEE MERCADO VERSUS LEONG (1996) 43 CAL. APP. 4TH 317, 324. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: JOANN PRIOR, CSR 129

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-21-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS 1905 LAGUNA STREET CONDOMINIUM HOMEOWNERS', MARK TANGARD, STEVE BARTHOLOMEW AND JOHN KEATING'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: 1. OVERRULED AS TO THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED AN ON-GOING CONTRACTUAL OBLIGATION. SEE ROMANO VERSUS ROCKWELL INTERNATIONAL INC., 14 CAL. 4TH 479, 489 (1996). 2. OVERRULED AS TO FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION. NEGLIGENCE ALLEGED TO BE ON-GOING AND DISTINCT EVENTS. AT BEST, ONLY PART OF THE CLAIM IS BARRED. 3. SUSTAIN WITH LEAVE TO AMEND THE TENTH AND ELEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION TO ALLEGE FACTS RELATED TO DEFENDANTS' BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY. CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 425.15 DOES APPLY TO THE ASSOCIATION. 4. SUSTAIN WITH LEAVE TO AMEND THE NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION. CIVIL CODE SECTION 1941 IS INAPPLICABLE; CIVIL CODE SECTION 1964 DOES NOT EXIST; PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS ESTABLISHING A BASIS FOR A CLAIM OF NUISANCE. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: JOANN PRIOR, CSR 129
